name: HMAS ML 815 (ML 815)
events: 18 Aug 1943 USS Billfish (Cdr. F.C. Lucas, Jr., USN) arried at Port Darwin. She was escorted in by HMAS ML 815 (T/Lt. C.A.J. Inman, RANVR).

19 May 1944 USS Crevalle (Cdr. F.D. Walker, Jr., USN) arrived at Darwin, Australia where her passengers were disembarked. Upon arrival she was escorted in by HMAS ML 815 (T/Lt. F.J. Horsfield, RANVR). She also underwent some minor repairs before departing for Fremantle the following day.

21 Dec 1944 During 21/22 December 1944, USS Besugo (T/Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN), USS Blackfin (T/Lt.Cdr. C.L. Raguet, USN) and USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN) conducted convoy attack exercises off Fremantle. These included night exercises.

The simulated convoy was made up of the frigate USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and the submarine rescue vessel USS Coucal (T/Lt. W.P. Baron, USN). They were escorted by the minesweepers HMAS Dubbo (T/Lt. F.W. Roberts, RANR(S)), HMAS Inverell (Lt. A.I. Chapman, RANR(S)), HMAS Mildura (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Little, RANR(S)), HMAS Warrnambool (Lt.Cdr.(Emgy.) E.J.B. Wight, RAN), armed yacht USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR), motor minesweepers USS YMS 11 (Lt. D.C. Maughan, USNR), USS YMS 72 (Lt. J.E. Reilly, USNR) and motor launches HMAS ML 815 (Lt. A.C. McAllister, RANVR) and HMAS ML 826 (Lt. S.A. Hancock, RANR).
Before commencement of the night exercises both motor minesweepers and both motor launches parted company. (1)

5 Jul 1945 Around 1800 H/5, HMS Taurus (A/Lt.Cdr. P.E. Newstead, DSC, RN) arrived at Fremantle from Trincomalee. She was escorted in by HMAS ML 815 (Lt. S.J. Davis, RANVR) which had joined around 1600 H/5.
HMS Taurus arrived at Fremantle with some engine defects for which she was taken in hand. (2)
20 Jul 1945 Around 2315 A/20, HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. P.J.S. de Jong, RNN) ended her 24th war patrol (12th in the Far East and 2nd for this deployment) at Fremantle.
She was escorted in by HMAS ML 815 (Lt. S.J. Davis, RANVR) which had joined 2125 A/20. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
